resolvedPathErrors.resolution: Added trailing period to match the CDI <name> exactly. Verified consistent with section-descriptions.yaml, field-descriptions.yaml, and recipes.yaml.

augmentedPaths.note: Not errors — these are synthetic disambiguation suffixes used by profile-1 to split a single CDI group into separate Producer/Consumer entries (Occupancy is dual-role; Turnout Control and Signal Controls contain a mix of (P) and (C) child events). The base CDI path before the parenthetical suffix exists in the registry. Consumers of this file should strip the suffix to look up the underlying group.

augmentedPaths.note: Not errors — the inner replicated group under 'Signal LED Brightness' has no <name> in the CDI, only four <repname> entries (Signal A, Signal B, Signal C, Signal D). 'Signal' is a synthesized template name used consistently across section-descriptions.yaml and field-descriptions.yaml to describe the template once. relevance-rules.json refers to a specific instance with the repname-based path 'Hardware Configuration/Signal LED Brightness/Signal D', which is a valid instance reference.

augmentedPaths.note: Not errors — Recipe 6 uses informal range descriptions to instruct the user to copy/paste multiple event IDs at once (avoiding 16 separate steps for the lamp-status events). Recipe 5 uses '<Remote node: …>' placeholders that intentionally point outside this CDI (the user must select a producer slot on a different LCC node). These are guidance text, not strict CDI references.

summary: PASS — 0 path errors, 0 enum errors, 100% coverage (3/3 segments, 17/17 groups, 17/17 non-event leaf fields, 61/61 event slots all described), 6/6 event-bearing groups have role classifications. The previous R001 path error (missing trailing period on 'How this TurnoutBoss is used on your layout.') has been corrected — see resolvedPathErrors. Three categories of non-error augmented paths are noted (see augmentedPaths): (1) profile-1's intentional (Produced)/(Consumed) suffixes that split mixed/dual-role groups; (2) the synthesized 'Signal' template name for the unnamed replicated brightness group; (3) recipes.yaml's informal multi-path range descriptions in Recipes 5 and 6. Profile is structurally valid and ready for use.
